lifecycle_current: {'lifecycle_current': 'evolved', 'notes': 'Intel LaGrande Technology (LT) was the code name for what became Intel Trusted Execution Technology (Intel TXT). The technology shipped as Intel TXT, integrated into vPro platforms. LaGrande as a name/codename was never a shipping product; it evolved into Intel TXT which remains active in current platforms.', 'source': 'https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Trusted_Execution_Technology'}
notes: Intel hardware-based trusted computing platform; became Intel TXT (Trusted Execution Technology). Aimed initially at business; Aberdeen proposes expanding to consumer segment. LT Platform = LT + OS + applications.

lifecycle_current: {'lifecycle_current': 'active', 'notes': 'TPM 2.0 required for Windows 11. Active standard-current status. Known answer applied.', 'source': 'known'}
notes: Hardware security component associated with LaGrande/TCG trusted computing ecosystem.

lifecycle_current: {'lifecycle_current': 'discontinued', 'notes': "The 'Consumer LT Platform' was a proposed 2003-era Intel concept for bringing LaGrande Technology (trusted computing) to consumer PCs. It was never shipped as a distinct consumer platform. The technology evolved into Intel TXT for enterprise/vPro use only; a separate consumer-focused version was not released.", 'source': 'known - concept never commercialized as proposed'}
notes: Aberdeen-proposed consumer version of LT Platform = LT hardware + OS + security applications. Estimated $100 retail uplift over standard PC; targeting identity theft/privacy concerns.
